original_description: Start here before writing any code — sizes task (S/L/H/Fix), sets up branch and session rules. Use when: "I'm starting on X", "quick fix for Y", "continuing from yesterday", "hotfix needed", "let's implement X", "skip to coding", "我要開始做 X", "快速修一下", "接續昨天的進度", resuming a feature branch, or any task that touches code. Not for: debugging (→ systematic-debugging), writing plans (→ writing-plans), brainstorming (→ brainstorming), or code review (→ quality-pipeline).
best_description: Start here before writing any code — sizes task (S/L/H/Fix), sets up branch and session rules. Use when: "I'm starting on X", "quick fix for Y", "continuing from yesterday", "hotfix needed", "let's implement X", "skip to coding", "我要開始做 X", "快速修一下", "接續昨天的進度", resuming a feature branch, or any task that touches code. Not for: debugging (→ systematic-debugging), writing plans (→ writing-plans), brainstorming (→ brainstorming), or code review (→ quality-pipeline).
final_description: Use before starting or resuming any coding work: size the task (S/L/H/Fix), set up your branch, establish session context. For features, quick fixes, hotfixes, continuing from previous sessions, or need to scope before coding. Not for: debugging issues, planning/design, brainstorming, or code reviews.

history.description: Start here before writing any code — sizes task (S/L/H/Fix), sets up branch and session rules. train_results.query: this endpoint returns 500 when the request body has a null broker_account_id. Can you trace through the handler and find the bug?
create a detailed plan for migrating our notification system from polling to SSE. I want architecture diagrams, phase breakdown, risk analysis.
review the code in my last 3 commits on this branch — check for security issues, missing error handling, and consistency with our patterns
I'm starting on the new webhook retry logic. It touches support/executors, the scheduler, and needs a new config table. Let me size this before jumping in.
ok let's start implementing the monthly settlement cron job. the plan is already in docs/plans/2026-03-21-allowance-distribution-engine.md, skip planning and go straight to coding
continuing work on feature/cross-tenant-allowance from yesterday. I left off at phase 3 of the plan.

test_results.query: write integration tests for the allowance engine — cover tier policy, split policy, and the cross-tenant edge case where tenant A owes tenant B
quick fix — the date format in the CSV export header is wrong, just need to change the strftime pattern in one file
production is down — the scheduler is panic'ing on startup because the advisory lock table doesn't exist after the migration rollback. hotfix needed NOW
let's brainstorm different approaches for the real-time P&L dashboard. maybe SSE, maybe websockets, maybe polling with smart caching?
